figures.caption: Fig. 4: H2. Energy achieved by each PWMCTS variant.
Fig. 3: Average GNN estimations M̂2 in the search tree.
Fig. 5: H2O. Energy achieved by each PWMCTS variant.
Fig. 6: LiH. Energy achieved by each PWMCTS variant.
Fig. 7: Exact M2 values and trace of the QFI matrix of the PQCs designed by each gradient-free PWMCTS variant.
TABLE III: Summary of the structure of PQCs designed by magic-informed variants against the PWMCTS baseline.
Fig. 8: Ground-state energy problem. Exact M2 values and trace of the QFI matrix of the PQCs designed by each gradient-free PWMCTS variant.
Fig. 9: Quantum state approximation. The bars report the median values of the fidelity achieved by each PWMCTS variant over 10 independent runs for all the nine target circuits.
Fig. 1: Graph Neural Network performance. On the left, the performances in terms of M2 estimations, on the right, in terms of Spearman’s ranking correlation.

Fig. 2: Generation procedure of the target states. For each value of the number of qubits n, the plot shows the M2 value distribution of the generated random quantum circuits (grey), of the resulting circuits optimized in M2 using PWMCTS with a number of iterations fixed to 500 (blue), and 2000 (red).
